label: false-dup
a: Paginated endpoints use cursor-based pagination: the client passes a cursor (encoded last-seen ID) and receives the next page. No OFFSET queries — all pagination is via index-range lookups.
b: Paginated API responses do not include a total count of results. Total count queries (SELECT COUNT(*)) are expensive on large tables and rarely needed by clients who paginate forward. Clients receive has_next_page instead.
reviewer_notes: Both about pagination but different facets: A is the pagination mechanism (cursors, no OFFSET); B is the omission of total-count. Either could exist independently.
